headline: MoE-certified L1 (layer-0 representative scope); whole-model headline L0
headline_note: Per ADR-007 the bare word 'certified' is forbidden for MoE. This family is 'MoE-certified L1' on the layer-0 representative scope (C1 exhaustive over layer-0's 60 experts + C2 layer-0 router, both on the REAL trained weights), with C3 satisfied by the existing attention mechanism certificate. Whole-model L1 (all 24 layers + real-weight attention) is pending the all-layers extension; until then the whole-model headline stays L0 (topology-certified). This is NOT the dense ADR-004 CERTIFIED guarantee.

C1_per_expert.scope: layer 0 — all 60 routed experts (exhaustive, not sampled)
C1_per_expert.weights: real trained Qwen1.5-MoE-A2.7B layer-0 weights (bf16 on disk)
C1_per_expert.oracle: NumPy float64, one expert at a time (no full-model F64)
C1_per_expert.metric: max_abs_diff(Atenia expert forward, F64 reference) per expert
C1_per_expert.gate: max_abs_diff < 0.5 for EVERY expert (ADR-004 bar, unchanged)
C1_per_expert.shared_expert_note: the always-on shared expert is exercised by the existing block/convention cert; C1 here covers the 60 routed experts.

C2_router.hard_gate: top-k expert SET EQUALITY vs the F64 reference
C2_router.routing_margin_note: gap between the 4th and 5th router logit (fragility signal, not a gate); Atenia and the F64 reference agree to <1e-2.

C3_attention.evidence: existing Qwen-MoE full-forward certificate — GQA + Q/K/V bias vs HF f64 = 5.960e-08 (MOE-FULL-13, docs/HANDOFF_MOE_FULL_13.md, tests/moe_certification_test.rs)
C3_attention.caveat: certifies the attention MECHANISM on a representative tiny fixture, NOT the real 14.3B attention weights re-certified per layer. Recorded, not over-claimed.

C4_assembly_topology.note: certifies routing+combine+assembly mechanism on a reduced-dim full-F64 fixture (random weights), not the real trained weights.

C5_active_path.blocker: real-weight active-path forward vs F64-active (~22 GB) or F32 cross-framework (~11 GB) not yet run (MOE-CERT-4).

pending_levels.blocker: extend C1 (per-expert) + C2 (router) from layer 0 to all 24 layers; real-weight C3 attention per layer.
fold C4 (topology, have it) with the whole-model L1 above.
C5 active-path (F32 cross-framework feasible on 32 GB; MOE-CERT-4).
global model.double() F64 ~114 GB — reserved / unreachable on the project hardware envelope; never fabricated.

verification.reference: fixtures/moe/qwen_moe_decomp_ref.safetensors (+ .json), computed in float64 one expert at a time
verification.expected: C1 worst max_abs_diff ~1.2e-7 (< 0.5, all 60 experts); C2 top-k [10,24,39,42] set match; routing margin 0.160333.
verification.ci_smoke: cert2_harness_mechanics_smoke (non-ignored) exercises the harness on the committed tiny fixture without the 27 GB model.

generated_on: F64 reference: PyTorch (bf16->float64) + NumPy float64, Windows 11. Atenia: CPU forward, RTX 4070 Laptop host, 32 GB RAM.
